**From VC**: Self-healing state machine (HEALTHY → SELF_HEALING → ESCALATED). ~200 lines.

**Gas Town implementation**: Preflight molecule or refinery feature:
```yaml
preflight:
  gates: [test, lint, build]
  on_failure: create-fix-issue
```

Run at session start. If baseline broken, file a P0 fix issue and work on that first.

**Value**: Self-healing baseline. Agents don't start from broken state.

**VC lesson**: Prevents cascading failures.  {"id":"gt-62hm","title":"Molecule Phase Terminology Documentation","description":"Document the three-phase molecule lifecycle with consistent terminology:

## The Phases (States of Matter)

| Phase | State | Nature |
|-------|-------|--------|
| **Proto** | Solid/Crystal | Frozen template, static definition in catalog |
| **Mol** | Liquid | Reified instance, malleable, tracked in git (durable) |
| **Wisp** | Gas | Evaporates after squash, ephemeral orchestration |

## Documentation Gaps Identified

### High Priority
1. Update vision.md Steam Engine metaphor to use proto/mol/wisp terminology
2. Add lifecycle diagram: Proto → bond → Mol or Wisp → squash → Digest
3. Document Go types for phases (ProtoMolecule, etc.)
4. Document .beads-ephemeral/ structure and purpose

### Medium Priority
5. Update CLAUDE.md files (polecat, witness, refinery) with molecule workflow
6. Document bd mol bond/squash/burn CLI API with examples
7. Add polecat guide: Executing molecules and generating summaries

### Low Priority
8. Add terminology glossary to architecture.md
9. Create troubleshooting playbook for stuck molecules
10. Add reference diagrams for state transitions

## Current State
- architecture.md has good molecule basics but uses inconsistent terminology
- vision.md has Steam Engine metaphor (proto=fuel, wisp=steam, digest=distillate)
- molecules.md has detailed reference but needs phase clarity
- builtin_molecules.go has 8 molecules but no phase documentation in code","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:32:27.537487-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:32:27.537487-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-662","title":"Swarm: report generation","description":"Generate markdown reports for completed swarms.

## Command
```
gt swarm report <swarm-id> [--save <file>]
```

## Report Content

### Header
- Swarm ID and title
- Created/completed timestamps
- Duration
- Rig name

### Task Summary
| Task | Assignee | Status | Duration |
|------|----------|--------|----------|
| gt-xxx | Toast | merged | 15m |
| gt-yyy | Nux | merged | 22m |

### Worker Contributions
- Commits per worker
- Issues closed per worker
- Lines changed (optional)

### Timeline
- Chronological events from events.jsonl
- Key milestones (started, first merge, landing)

### Issues Encountered
- Conflicts resolved
- Failed tasks (if any)
- Escalations

## Implementation
```go
func GenerateReport(swarmID string) (*SwarmReport, error)
func (r *SwarmReport) ToMarkdown() string
```

## Storage
- Save to <rig>/.gastown/swarms/<id>/report.md
- Or user-specified path with --save

## PGT Reference
gastown-py/src/gastown/swarm/manager.py generate_report()

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Markdown report generated
- [ ] Includes all sections above
- [ ] Auto-saved to swarm directory
- [ ] --save allows custom path","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:17.96767-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:22.330075-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:22.330075-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-6957","title":"Deacon primes pending workers instead of spawn nudge","description":"## Problem

When gt spawn starts a polecat, it tries to send a nudge message immediately:
1. Starts tmux session with Claude Code
2. Waits 3 seconds (hardcoded)
3. Sends nudge via NudgeSession

But Claude Code takes 10-20+ seconds to be ready. The message arrives before
the prompt is ready, so it appears as part of the prompt string instead of
being executed.

## Solution

Move priming responsibility to the Deacon patrol cycle:

1. gt spawn: Mark polecat as pending_prime=true in state, return immediately
2. Deacon patrol: New step prime-pending-workers that:
   - Scans all rigs for workers with pending_prime=true
   - Uses WaitForClaudeReady() to poll for "> " prompt (already exists in tmux.go)
   - Once ready, sends gt prime + assignment nudge
   - Clears pending_prime flag

## Implementation

### spawn.go changes
- Remove the 3-second sleep (line 368)
- Remove the NudgeSession call (lines 375-383)
- Instead: Update polecat state with pending_prime=true

### Polecat state addition
status: assigned, pending_prime: true, issue: gt-xxx

### Deacon patrol addition
Add step between inbox-check and plugin-run:
prime-pending-workers: Check all rigs for workers marked pending_prime.
For each pending worker: WaitForClaudeReady(60s), then gt prime + nudge

### Benefits
- No race condition - we poll until actually ready
- Deacon already does health checks - priming is natural fit
- Works for polecats, witnesses, refineries, crew (unified approach)
- Timeout handling built in

## Depends on
- Existing WaitForClaudeReady() in tmux.go (lines 384-403)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T17:19:33.589909-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T17:31:27.458838-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T17:31:27.458838-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented: spawn no longer nudges, deacon patrol has trigger-pending-spawns step"}

 {"id":"gt-7918","title":"Patrols: Cyclic molecules for autonomous maintenance","description":"## Vision

Patrols are **cyclic molecules** - workflow loops that give Gas Town its autonomous nervous system. While regular molecules are DAGs that terminate, patrols loop forever, performing maintenance tasks at varying cadences.

This is the "steam engine" of Gas Town: converting episodic Claude sessions into continuous autonomous operation.

## Core Concepts

### 1. Cyclic Molecules

Regular molecule:     A -> B -> C -> done
Patrol molecule:      A -> B -> C --+
                      ^             |
                      +-------------+

Patrols have a loop_to field specifying where to restart.

### 2. Cooldown-Aware Steps (Atoms)

Steps encode their own cadence. When patrol reaches a step:
1. Check: now - last_run > cooldown?
2. If yes: execute, update last_run
3. If no: skip (immediately close)

The patrol runner is simple - steps self-skip. Complexity distributed into atoms.

### 3. The Beacon

The heartbeat that fires patrol triggers:
- Internal ticker in Deacon (goroutine)
- Or external cron firing gt deacon tick
- Or mail-based triggers

Without beacon, nothing proactive happens.

### 4. Session Reset as Patrol Step

Connects to auto-handoff (gt-bcwn). Session reset is a patrol step, not a separate mechanism.

### 5. Multi-Role Patrols

Each supervisor has its own patrol:

**Deacon patrol:** health-check (30s), session-gc (5m), beacon-tick (10s)
**Witness patrol:** orphan-scan (10m), stuck-check (2m), molecule-progress (1m)
**Refinery patrol:** queue-check (30s), pr-status (1m), merge-ready (30s)

### 6. Cadence Tiers

- Critical (10-30s): Health checks
- Active (1-5m): Progress, nudges
- Maintenance (10-30m): Orphans, GC
- Periodic (1h+): Reports

### 7. Best-Effort Scheduling

Not real-time - more like cron. No hard deadlines. Catch-up, dont pile-up.
Priority preemption (mail interrupts patrol). Graceful degradation under load.

## Open Questions

1. State persistence: Beads (self-describing) or file (faster)?
2. Interruption: How does urgent mail preempt patrol?
3. Error recovery: Backoff? Escalate? Circuit breaker?
4. Coordination: Can patrols send mail to trigger other patrols?

## Related

- gt-bcwn: Auto-handoff (session reset is a patrol step)
- Molecule system (patrols extend molecules with loops)
- Deacon lifecycle management

## Metaphor

Claude was fire. Claude Code was steam. Gas Town is the steam engine. Beads is the train tracks.

The steam engine converts episodic combustion into continuous rotary motion.
Gas Town converts episodic Claude sessions into continuous autonomous work.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:18:22.99128-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:18:22.99128-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-7918","depends_on_id":"gt-bcwn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:18:30.86651-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-7920","title":"Create mol-refinery-patrol with test failure gates","description":"## Problem

The Refinery role lacks:
1. A formal patrol molecule (Deacon has mol-deacon-patrol)
2. Engineer identity/philosophy guidance
3. Structural enforcement of failure handling (not just documentation)

When tests fail during merge processing, the current guidance doesn't prevent 'disavowal' - noting a problem exists and proceeding without tracking it.

## The Structural Solution

Create `mol-refinery-patrol` with **verification gates** that make disavowal structurally impossible:

```markdown
## Molecule: mol-refinery-patrol

## Step: inbox-check
Check mail for MR submissions, escalations, messages.
Process any urgent items first.

## Step: queue-scan
Fetch remote, identify polecat branches waiting.
If queue empty, skip to context-check.
Track branch list for this cycle.
Needs: inbox-check

## Step: process-branch
Pick next branch. Rebase on current main.
If rebase conflicts: notify polecat, skip to next branch.
Needs: queue-scan

## Step: run-tests
Run go test ./...
Track results (pass/fail count).
Needs: process-branch

## Step: handle-failures
**GATE**: If tests passed, this step auto-completes.
If tests failed:
- Diagnose: branch regression or pre-existing?
- Branch regression → abort, notify polecat
- Pre-existing → EITHER fix OR \`bd create --type=bug\`
**VERIFY**: Fix committed OR bead filed before proceeding.
Needs: run-tests

## Step: merge-push
Merge to main (ff-only preferred).
Push immediately.
Delete polecat branch.
Needs: handle-failures

## Step: loop-check
More branches? Return to process-branch.
Otherwise continue to summary.
Needs: merge-push

## Step: generate-summary
Summarize cycle: branches processed, tests results, issues filed.
Needs: loop-check

## Step: context-check
Check context usage.
If high: write handoff, prepare for burn/respawn.
Needs: generate-summary

## Step: burn-or-loop
If context LOW and queue non-empty: loop (return to queue-scan)
If context HIGH or queue empty: burn and exit
Needs: context-check
```

## Implementation Tasks

### 1. Add RefineryPatrolMolecule() to builtin_molecules.go
- Copy pattern from DeaconPatrolMolecule
- Include all steps with proper `Needs:` dependencies
- Emphasize handle-failures gate in description

### 2. Update Refinery CLAUDE.md
Add sections:
- **The Engineer Mindset** (identity/philosophy)
- **The Scotty Test** (Would Scotty walk past this?)
- **Patrol Molecule** reference (mol-refinery-patrol)
- **Test Failure Protocol** (explicit decision tree)

### 3. Create prompts/roles/refinery.md
- Follow pattern of deacon.md
- Include patrol execution loop diagram
- Reference the molecule

### 4. Consider mol-witness-patrol
- Witness has detailed heartbeat protocol but no formal molecule
- Similar pattern could apply

## Why This Matters

GUPP: 'If you have work on your hook, you have to run it.'
The patrol molecule puts failure handling ON THE HOOK as a mandatory step.
You can't skip handle-failures to get to merge-push.
The structure enforces good behavior.

## Related
- gt-7919: Fix failing beads tests (discovered during this session)
- mol-deacon-patrol (existing pattern to follow)
- docs/molecular-chemistry.md (chemistry concepts)
- docs/wisp-architecture.md (wisp vs mol decision)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:01:48.588945-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T13:20:37.565029-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T13:13:36.329669-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-7921","title":"Add await-work and plugin-run steps to mol-refinery-patrol","description":"## Context

The mol-refinery-patrol needs two additional steps:

### 1. await-work (Decision Point)

Not just 'wait for signal' but a crossroads:
- Check for pending signals (mail, nudge, human)
- Check plugin gates (any plugins need to run?)
- Check maintenance schedule (daily/weekly due?)
- Evaluate context and decide next action

Position: Between burn-or-loop and inbox-check (the loop point)

### 2. plugin-run (Like Deacon)

Refinery needs plugin support for:
- **Monitors**: Flag PRs touching sensitive code
- **Gates**: Block merges under certain conditions
- **Schedulers**: Reorder queue based on priority/urgency
- **Maintenance**: Weekly cleanup, audits, stats
- **Audits**: Log merge statistics, track patterns

Position: After queue-scan, before process-branch

## Implementation

Update RefineryPatrolMolecule() in builtin_molecules.go to include:
- await-work step with decision tree
- plugin-run step with gate types

## Related
- gt-7920 (original mol-refinery-patrol)
- docs/deacon-plugins.md (existing plugin model)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:24:11.267237-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T13:24:11.267237-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-7922","title":"Dynamic wisp modification: insert, reorder, bond mid-execution","description":"## Vision

Wisps should be living documents that adapt mid-execution, not static scripts.

## Operations Needed

### bd mol insert
Insert a step into a running wisp:
```bash
bd mol insert <wisp-id> --after <step> --step 'security-gate: Review auth changes'
```

### bd mol reorder  
Change step order in a running wisp:
```bash
bd mol reorder <wisp-id> --move process-branch --before current
```

### bd mol bond (to wisp)
Attach an entire molecule to a running wisp:
```bash
bd mol bond mol-weekly-maintenance <wisp-id> --after await-work
```

### bd mol skip
Skip a step (with reason):
```bash
bd mol skip <wisp-id> <step> --reason 'Queue empty, skipping summary'
```

## Use Cases

1. **Hotfix priority**: Reorder queue to process urgent MR first
2. **Security gate**: Insert review step when PR touches sensitive files
3. **Scheduled maintenance**: Bond maintenance mol on schedule
4. **Context adaptation**: Skip steps when they don't apply

## Why This Matters

Engine room problems emerge dynamically. The wisp's flexibility lets agents model situations as they evolve, not execute rigid scripts.

GUPP still applies: whatever's on your hook, you run. But the hook contents can adapt.

## Implementation

This requires Beads changes to support wisp mutation:
- Track step ordering in wisp storage
- Support step insertion/deletion/reordering
- Handle bond-to-wisp (spawn steps into existing wisp)
- Preserve audit trail of modifications","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:24:26.533247-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T13:24:26.533247-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-7we","title":"Swarms of One: Lightweight single-worker task dispatch","description":"Design and implement a lightweight pattern for firing off single workers to handle tasks without full swarm overhead.

## Context

Currently we have:
- town spawn: Creates a polecat in a rig with issue assignment
- Swarms (sw-*): Full lifecycle tracking with manifest, state, events, reports
- Ephemeral rigs (rig-*): Temporary worker groups for swarms

What's missing: A simple way to say 'fire off a worker to do X' without swarm ceremony.

## Design Questions

1. Should this be a new command like 'gt fire' or an option on existing commands?
2. Should single tasks still get swarm IDs (sw-N) for consistency/queryability?
3. Should it default to creating new workers or support --reuse for idle polecats?
4. How does this relate to crew workers (gt-cik)?

## Possible Interface

gt fire --rig gastown --issue gt-xyz [--prompt '...']
gt fire gastown/QuickTask --issue gt-xyz

## Related

- gt-cik: Overseer Crew (user-managed persistent workspaces)
- gt-kmn: Swarm System epic","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T16:51:02.716629-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:18.589027-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:18.589027-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-82y","title":"Design: Swarm shutdown and worker cleanup","description":"Design for graceful swarm shutdown, worker cleanup, and session cycling.

## Key Decisions

1. Pre-kill verification uses model intelligence (not framework rules)
2. Witness can request restart when context filling (mail self, exit)
3. Mayor NOT involved in per-worker cleanup (Witness responsibility)
4. Clear responsibility boundaries between Mayor/Witness/Polecat

## Subtasks (implementation)

- gt-sd6: Polecat decommission checklist prompting
- gt-f8v: Witness pre-kill verification protocol
- gt-eu9: Witness session cycling and handoff
- gt-gl2: Mayor vs Witness cleanup responsibilities

**Design complete.** Each subtask has full specification in its description.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:47:44.936374-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:49:22.849598-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T20:12:05.441911-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-8bx","title":"Adaptive backoff for daemon heartbeat","description":"Track agent responsiveness and adjust heartbeat frequency.

## Per-Agent State

```go
type AgentBackoff struct {
    BaseInterval    time.Duration  // 60s default
    CurrentInterval time.Duration  // grows when busy
    MaxInterval     time.Duration  // 10min cap
    ConsecutiveMiss int            // pokes with no response
}
```

## Strategy Options

- **Fixed**: Always 60s (current, simple)
- **Geometric**: 60s → 90s → 135s → 202s (factor 1.5)
- **Exponential**: 60s → 120s → 240s (factor 2, aggressive)

## Recovery

When agent responds (runs a command):
- Reset ConsecutiveMiss to 0
- Return to BaseInterval immediately

## Benefits

- Reduces noise for busy agents
- Saves resources during quiet periods
- Still catches stuck agents (max interval cap)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:33.083844-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:09:31.613912-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:09:31.613912-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8bx","depends_on_id":"gt-bfd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:46.912289-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-gby","title":"gt handoff: Unified agent lifecycle command","description":"## Summary

Unified `gt handoff` command for ALL agent types to request lifecycle actions.

## Usage

gt handoff              # Context-aware default
gt handoff --shutdown   # Terminate, cleanup, don't restart
gt handoff --cycle      # Restart with handoff mail
gt handoff --restart    # Fresh restart, no handoff

## Context-Aware Defaults

| Agent Type | Default | Reason |
|------------|---------|--------|
| Polecat | --shutdown | Ephemeral, work is done |
| Witness | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Refinery | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Mayor | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Crew | (sends mail only) | Human-managed |

## What gt handoff Does

1. **Verify safe to stop**
   - Git state clean (no uncommitted changes)
   - Work handed off (PR exists for polecats)

2. **Send handoff mail to self** (for cycle/restart)
   - Captures current state
   - New session will read this

3. **Send lifecycle request to manager**
   - Polecats/Refinery → Witness
   - Witness/Mayor → Daemon
   - Format: mail to <manager> with action type

4. **Set state: requesting_<action>**
   - Lifecycle manager checks this before acting

5. **Wait for termination**
   - Don't self-exit - let manager kill session
   - Ensures clean handoff

## Lifecycle Request Flow

Agent                           Lifecycle Manager
  |                                    |
  | 1. gt handoff --cycle              |
  |    a. Verify git clean             |
  |    b. Send handoff mail to self    |
  |    c. Set requesting_cycle=true    |
  |    d. Send lifecycle request       |
  |------------------------------------→|
  |                                    |
  |                   2. Receive request
  |                   3. Verify state  |
  |                   4. Kill session  |
  |                   5. Start new     |
  |                      (for cycle)   |
  |                                    |
  |         New session reads handoff  |
  |         Resumes work               |

## Who Manages Whom

| Agent | Sends lifecycle request to |
|-------|---------------------------|
| Polecat | <rig>/witness |
| Refinery | <rig>/witness |
| Witness | daemon/ |
| Mayor | daemon/ |

## Implementation

1. Detect current role (polecat, witness, refinery, mayor, crew)
2. Apply context-aware default if no flag specified
3. Run pre-flight checks (git clean, work handed off)
4. Send handoff mail to self (if cycling)
5. Send lifecycle request to appropriate manager
6. Set requesting_<action> in state.json
7. Wait (manager will kill us)

## For Polecats (--shutdown)

Additional cleanup after kill:
- Witness removes worktree
- Witness deletes polecat branch
- Polecat ceases to exist

## Related Issues

- gt-99m: Daemon (handles Mayor/Witness lifecycle)
- gt-7ik: Ephemeral polecats (polecat cleanup)
- gt-eu9: Witness session cycling","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:40.806863-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:18:22.35369-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-7ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:46.423945-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-eu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:46.547204-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:50:24.142182-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-gl2","title":"Clarify Mayor vs Witness cleanup responsibilities","description":"Document the cleanup authority model: Witness owns ALL per-worker cleanup, Mayor never involved.

## The Rule

**Witness handles ALL per-worker cleanup. Mayor is never involved.**

## Why This Matters

1. Separation of concerns: Mayor strategic, Witness operational
2. Reduced coordination overhead: No back-and-forth for routine cleanup
3. Faster shutdown: Witness kills workers immediately upon verification
4. Cleaner escalation: Mayor only hears about problems

## What Witness Handles

- Verifying worker git state before kill
- Nudging workers to fix dirty state
- Killing worker sessions
- Updating worker state (sleep/wake)
- Logging verification results

## What Mayor Handles

- Receiving swarm complete notifications
- Deciding whether to start new swarms
- Handling escalations (stuck workers after 3 retries)
- Cross-rig coordination

## Escalation Path

Worker stuck -> Witness nudges (up to 3x) -> Witness escalates to Mayor -> Mayor decides: force kill, reassign, or human

## Anti-Patterns

DO NOT: Mayor asks Witness if worker X is clean
DO: Witness reports swarm complete, all workers verified

DO NOT: Mayor kills worker sessions directly
DO: Mayor tells Witness to abort swarm, Witness handles cleanup

DO NOT: Workers report done to Mayor
DO: Workers report to Witness, Witness aggregates and reports up","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:48:56.678724-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:12.068964-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-gl2","depends_on_id":"gt-82y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:49:05.929877-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-h262","title":"bd ready --blockers: prioritize issues that block other work","description":"bd ready should prioritize issues that are blocking other work.

**From VC**: Blocker-first prioritization in GetReadyWork(). ~100 lines.
Algorithm: baseline-failure first, then discovered:blocker, then by priority.

**Gas Town implementation**: CLI flag or default behavior:
```bash
bd ready --blockers-first  # Or make this default
```

Checks dependency graph. Issues with many dependents surface first.

**Value**: Unblocks parallelism faster. Critical path gets cleared.

**VC lesson**: Without blocker priority, work can starve on discovered issues.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:18.426957-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:18.426957-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-h262","depends_on_id":"gt-zhpa","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:27.664473-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-h28m","title":"Deacon patrol banners: visual feedback on atom transitions","description":"Print large ASCII banners when transitioning between patrol atoms.

## Problem

When the deacon progresses through patrol atoms (steps), there is no visual feedback.
The operator cannot easily see what the deacon is doing without reading the full output.

## Desired Behavior

Print banners on step start and completion:

  INBOX-CHECK - Checking for lifecycle requests, escalations, timers
  INBOX-CHECK COMPLETE - Processed 3 messages, 0 lifecycle requests

## Benefits

1. Scanability: Operator can glance at tmux and see what is happening
2. Progress tracking: Easy to see where in the patrol loop we are
3. Debugging: Clear demarcation between steps for troubleshooting

## Implementation Options

1. In deacon CLAUDE.md: Instruct agent to print banners
2. gt patrol step start/end: Commands that print banners
3. bd mol step hooks: Automatically on step transitions

## Related

- gt-id36: Deacon Kernel
- gt-rana: Patrol System","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T03:04:14.290474-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:54:55.972256-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:54:55.972256-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented in deacon.md.tmpl - startup banner, step banners with emojis, cycle summary banner.  {"id":"gt-lek6","title":"gt rig reset --stale: Clear orphaned in_progress items","description":"Reset in_progress issues when their assigned agent no longer exists.

## Problem
When polecats die without cleanup, their issues remain in_progress forever.
Need a way to bulk-reset these orphaned items.

## Command
```bash
gt rig reset --stale [--dry-run]
```

## Logic
For each in_progress issue in rig:
1. Parse assignee (e.g., "gastown/furiosa")
2. Map to tmux session name (gt-gastown-furiosa)
3. If session does NOT exist:
   - Reset status to "open"
   - Clear assignee
4. Exception: skip crew/* assignees (persistent identities)
   OR check if crew tmux session exists

## Output
```
Resetting stale work in gastown:
  gt-abc: gastown/furiosa (no session) → open
  gt-def: gastown/nux (no session) → open
  Skipped: gt-xyz: gastown/crew/max (persistent)
Reset 2 issues, skipped 1
```

## Related
- gt-2kz: CLI cleanup commands for stale state
- gt-rdmw: orphan-check in deacon patrol
- gt-orphans command (list orphaned molecules)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:33:46.962413-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:16:22.295127-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:16:22.295127-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented --stale flag for gt rig reset with dry-run support"}

 {"id":"gt-lno","title":"Swarm state persistence: manifest + state + events","description":"Upgrade swarm persistence to match PGT pattern.

## Current State
Single .gastown/swarms.json with all swarms.

## Target State (per-swarm)
```
.gastown/swarms/
└── <swarm-id>/
    ├── manifest.json  # Immutable config
    ├── state.json     # Mutable progress
    ├── events.jsonl   # Audit log
    └── report.md      # Generated report
```

## File Schemas

### manifest.json (immutable after creation)
```json
{
  "id": "sw-1",
  "title": "Epic description",
  "epic_id": "gt-abc",
  "rig": "gastown",
  "base_commit": "abc123",
  "integration_branch": "swarm/sw-1",
  "target_branch": "main",
  "workers": ["Toast", "Nux"],
  "tasks": [{"id": "gt-xyz", "title": "..."}],
  "created_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
}
```

### state.json (mutable)
```json
{
  "state": "active",
  "task_states": {
    "gt-xyz": {"state": "merged", "assignee": "Toast"}
  },
  "updated_at": "2024-01-01T01:00:00Z",
  "error": null
}
```

### events.jsonl (append-only audit)
```jsonl
{"event": "created", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
{"event": "task_assigned", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
{"event": "task_merged", "ts": "...", "data": {...}}
```

## Benefits
- Audit trail via events.jsonl
- Manifest immutability prevents corruption
- Cleaner separation of concerns
- Per-swarm isolation

## Migration
Keep backward compat with swarms.json during transition.

## Files to Modify
- internal/swarm/manager.go: Refactor persistence
- internal/cmd/swarm.go: SwarmStore → directory-based

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Per-swarm directory structure
- [ ] Events logged to JSONL
- [ ] Manifest immutable after creation
- [ ] List command aggregates from directories","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:14.151538-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:19.716405-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T17:23:19.716405-08:00"}
